english: pa-la (pa la) frq.; ṅó-tsa-la mi _dzém-pa (ngo tsha la mi 'dzem pa) Cs. insensible to shame, shameless; nád-rigs-la-mi _dzém-na (nad rigs la mi 'dzem na) unless one is on his guard against the several diseases; also to feel ashamed, *ṅe'-nam-la mi _dzem-mam* (*nge' nam la mi 'dzem mam*) C. do you not feel abashed in our presence? _dzém-pa-čan ('dzem pa can), _dzém-bag-čan ('dzem bag can) bashful, modest, temperate Cs.; _dzém(-pa)-med(-pa) ('dzem (-pa) med (-pa)) the contrary; krel-_dzém (khrel 'dzem) modesty Cs.

1. to say, to speak, Stg. 57, 6, obs., v. zér-ba (zer ba). — 2. to be hoarse, _dzér-po ('dzer po) hoarse, skad (skad) Dzl., Med.; skad _dzer-_dzér-du ṅú-ba (skad 'dzer 'dzer du ngu ba) to weep with a very hoarse voice Pth. — 3. to solder Sch.
pf. btsogs (btsogs), fut. btsog (btsog) to heap together, to jumble, to throw disorderly together Cs.
Ts. *_dzog-_dzóg* (*'dzog 'dzog*) 1. jagged, pointed, conical. — 2. oblong, cylindrical C.

to come together, to meet, *dzom tsár-ra ma tsar* (*dzom tsar ra ma tsar*) are they already assembled? dág-pa mṅón-dgai žiṅ-kams der _ó-skol _dzóm-par ɣdon mi za (dag pa mngon dga'i zhing khams der 'o skol 'dzom par gdon mi za) that we shall meet again in the realms of pure bliss, that is certain Mil.; tses bčo-lṅá daṅ _dzóms-pas (tshes bco lnga dang 'dzoms pas) as it just fell upon the 15th Glr.; *dzom mi dzom* (*dzom mi dzom*) W. they do not agree with each other; dé-rnams rnyéd-par dká-ste mi _dzom (de rnams rnyed par dka' ste mi 'dzom) as it is difficult to obtain these things, we shall not be able to get all of them together Glr.; *dzóm-pa mé'-pa čig kyaṅ mé'* (*dzom pa me' pa cig kyang me'*) C. there is nothing that does not find its way there, that is not to be had there; to be plentiful Mil.; as partic. with termin. case: rich in, abounding Mil. — dál-byor _dzóm-pai lus (dal byor 'dzom pa'i lus) Mil. v. dál-ba (dal ba). — kun-_dzóm (kun 'dzom) 'where all meet', name of mountain-passes, e.g. between Lh. and Sp., and of females; in a similar manner gaṅ-_dzóm (gang 'dzom) and byor-_dzóm (byor 'dzom) ('conflux of goods'). — _dzóm-po ('dzom po) rich in C., rtsa-ču _dzón-po (rtsa chu 'dzon po) abounding in grass and water, fertile C.; mtun-rkyén _dzóm-po (mthun rkyen 'dzom po) fortunate, successful, through a favourable concurrence of circumstances; tsos-sna-_dzóm-po (tshos sna 'dzom po) variegated, many-coloured.
fault, error, mistake, dé-la _dzól-pa ɣsum byuṅ (de la 'dzol pa gsum byung) he fell into three mistakes, committed three errors Glr.

to shake about, to stir or shake up, e.g. a feather-bed; to confound, to confuse, prin goṅ-_og _dzol-ba (phrin gong 'og 'dzol ba) to deliver a message confusedly, making a mess of it Glr.; W.: *zol-zól co-če'* (*zol zol co ce'*). — *_dzól-tso* (*'dzol tsho*) C., *zol-zól* (*zol zol*) W. difference.

W. *za* (*za*), 1. clay, gen. rdzá-sa (rdza sa). — 2. in comp. for rdzá-ma (rdza ma), e.g. čáṅ-rdza (chang rdza) beer-jug, čú-rdza (chu rdza) water-pitcher Cs. — Comp. and deriv. rdza-kór (rdza kor) earthen bowl, little dish. — rdza-káṅ (rdza khang) pottery Schr. — rdza-kúṅ (rdza khung) clay-pit. — rdza-mkán (rdza mkhan) potter, rdza-mkán-gyi ₑkór-lo skor-ba (rdza mkhan gyi 'khor lo skor ba) to turn the potter's wheel Dom. — rdza-rṅá (rdza rnga) kettle-drum of burnt clay. — rdza-čág (rdza chag) potsherd. — rdza-čú (rdza chu), or more refined rdza-čáb (rdza chab), water issuing from clay-slate rocks Mil. and elsewh. — rdza-čén (rdza chen) a large, rdza-čúṅ (rdza chung) a little pot, v. rdzá-ma (rdza ma). — rdza-snód (rdza snod), rdza-spyád (rdza spyad) earthen vessel. — rdza-pág (rdza phag) tile, (Dutch) tile for stoves. — rdza-pór (rdza phor) C. = rdza-kór (rdza kor). — rdza-búm (rdza bum) 1. pitcher, jar, bottle, formed of clay. 2. jar, in gen., lčágs-kyi rdza-búm (lcags kyi rdza bum) iron jar Stg. — rdzá-bo (rdza bo) an earthen vessel Cs. — rdzá-ma (rdza ma) pot (unglazed, urn-shaped, bellied vessels of various size, not for cooking, but only for holding water, butter and the like). — rdza-ɣzóṅ (rdza gzong) earthen basin. — rdza-rí (rdza ri) mountain consisting of clay-slate. — dza-sá (rdza sa) argillaceous earth, clay. — dza-brá (rdza bra), C. *dzab-ra* (*dzab ra*), W. *zab-ra* (*zab ra*) a mole-like animal.
Mil., for dzó-gi (dzo gi), yó-gi (yo gi).
chest, box, for various store = báṅ-ba (bang ba) Thgy.
, dam-rdzáb (dam rdzab), mud, mire (Cs. clay); rdzab-dóṅ (rdzab dong) sink, slough.
sham, emptiness, falsehood, rmí-lam rdzab-rdzúb-čan (rmi lam rdzab rdzub can) an empty dream Cs.
